message:
chore(beads): clean up br_history snapshots and update issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -58,7 +58,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","title":"Redesign history timelines, milestone/event composition, and commit cards for fast investigation and chronology scanning","description":"Purpose:
Improve the core visual grammar of history mode so chronology and event significance are visible without reading dense text blocks.

Required scope:
- Rework event/timeline composition into a clearer rail or card-based chronology.
- Make milestone and lifecycle state changes more visually legible.
- Rebuild commit presentation so commit cards and change summaries are easier to scan and compare.

Acceptance:
- Chronology is easier to understand at a glance.
- Commit/event reading is faster and less text-fatiguing.
- The screen has stronger investigation rhythm than the current report-like output.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. History should read like a chronology tool, not a visually upgraded transcript.\n2. Event, milestone, and commit cards must remain comparable under dense timelines and history-rich projects.\n3. Sparse history, partial history, and ambiguous chronology need intentional presentation so the user understands what is missing.\n4. Responsive fallbacks must preserve time ordering and event significance even when there is no room for the richest layout.\n\nFuture-self note: If users still have to read line by line to reconstruct what happened, this bead is not succeeding.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Chronology is easier to understand at a glance.\n2. Commit/event reading is faster and less text-fatiguing across dense and sparse history cases.\n3. Partial or thin history is communicated honestly without destroying investigation rhythm.\n4. Snapshot, keyflow, and scenario-driven proof cover dense timelines, sparse histories, and responsive chronology layouts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.023159910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:26:01.744593257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","history","layout","timeline","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.023159910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:03.732591731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:04.184069676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","title":"Upgrade history file-tree, related-bead drilldowns, link affordances, and context-preserving jumps","description":"Purpose:
Strengthen the interactive workflows that make history mode worth using during real investigations.

Required scope:
- Improve file-tree interaction, visibility, and focus signaling.
- Make related-bead drilldowns clearer and easier to traverse.
- Use terminal links or other first-class affordances where they improve navigation to commits, issues, repos, or docs.
- Preserve and improve context when jumping between history and adjacent modes.

Acceptance:
- File-tree and related-bead workflows feel deliberate rather than bolted on.
- Link and jump behavior reduces context loss instead of increasing it.
- History mode becomes a reliable place to investigate cause and sequence.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. File-tree and related-bead workflows should reduce investigation cost, not add another navigation puzzle.\n2. Preserve context aggressively when jumping out and back, especially after filtering, confidence changes, or file narrowing.\n3. Interactive elements must remain usable under long paths, Unicode, dense file sets, and sparse result sets.\n4. Make it obvious when history evidence is partial or when a jump is leaving the current investigative frame.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is about investigation continuity. If users keep losing their place when drilling around, the rebuilt history mode still is not trustworthy.","acceptance_criteria":"1. File-tree and related-bead workflows feel deliberate rather than bolted on.\n2. Link and jump behavior reduces context loss instead of increasing it, including after filters and confidence changes.\n3. Dense, sparse, long-path, and partial-evidence cases remain usable and honest.\n4. Unit/state, hit-region, and journey proof cover drilldowns, link handoffs, jump-back continuity, and partial-history edge cases with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.390188565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:26:08.224099101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["files","frankentui","history","links","navigation","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.390188565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:05.465037595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:04.594486365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:05.032449046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","title":"Add FrankenTUI-driven layout diagnostics and deterministic visual-debug tooling for the TUI redesign","description":"Purpose:
Make the redesign debuggable. Complex responsive TUI work should not rely on guesswork and snapshots alone.

Background:
The FrankenTUI showcase stack includes stronger layout-debug and deterministic-visual-debug patterns than `bvr` currently uses. The redesign will touch information architecture, widths, splitters, and hit regions across many screens, so the project needs better developer tools before it scales the redesign too far.

Required scope:
- Introduce or emulate layout-debugging aids that help explain why panels receive the sizes they do.
- Add deterministic visual-debug hooks suitable for testing complex screen states and responsive transitions.
- Make it easier to inspect hit regions, pane boundaries, or screen-mode thresholds while developing redesign work.

Acceptance:
- Developers have better-than-current tools for diagnosing wasted space, clipping, and awkward screen composition.
- The redesign is no longer validated only by staring at final snapshots.
- The tooling is practical enough to be reused across multiple screen rebuilds.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Prefer reusable diagnostics that multiple redesign beads can share over one-off debug hacks.\n2. Focus on layout and constraint visibility, hit-region visibility, and deterministic visual-state capture.\n3. Tooling should make developers faster during redesign, not become a side project detached from the product.\n\nFuture-self note: Without this bead, later TUI work will still be debugged by intuition and screenshot diffing. That is too weak for the scale of redesign being planned here.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The TUI redesign has practical diagnostics for layout, pane, or hit-region reasoning.\n2. Deterministic debug capture exists for non-trivial screen states and responsive transitions.\n3. The tooling is documented enough to be reused by later maintainers.\n4. Tests cover the diagnostics where feasible so the tools themselves do not rot.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.772906382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:20:39.907726857Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","frankentui","layout","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.772906382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:59.452550804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:59.852160600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:05.891553480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","title":"Build layout and hit-region diagnostics for redesign work: pane maps, constraint visibility, and interaction overlays","description":"Purpose:
Create reusable diagnostics that expose where panes, constraints, and hit regions actually are during redesign work.

Required scope:
1. Make pane boundaries and hit regions inspectable enough to debug shell and screen interaction bugs.
2. Provide a practical way to inspect layout allocation and identify wasted or clipped space.
3. Keep the diagnostics lightweight enough that they can be used repeatedly during implementation.

Acceptance:
1. Developers can inspect layout and hit-region problems without guessing from screenshots alone.
2. The diagnostics are reusable across multiple redesigned screens.
3. Tests or deterministic checks cover the core diagnostic logic where feasible.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","frankentui","hittest","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Create reusable diagnostics that expose where panes, constraints, and hit regions actually are during redesign work.

Required scope:
1. Make pane boundaries and hit regions inspectable enough to debug shell and screen interaction bugs.
2. Provide a practical way to inspect layout allocation and identify wasted or clipped space.
3. Keep the diagnostics lightweight enough that they can be used repeatedly during implementation.

Acceptance:
1. Developers can inspect layout and hit-region problems without guessing from screenshots alone.
2. The diagnostics are reusable across multiple redesigned screens.
3. Tests or deterministic checks cover the core diagnostic logic where feasible.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:32:33.555921581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","frankentui","hittest","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.2","title":"Build deterministic visual-debug capture and responsive-state replay hooks for redesign verification","description":"Purpose:
Create reproducible visual-debug hooks so complex redesigned states can be captured and replayed reliably.

Required scope:
1. Support deterministic capture of important screen states and responsive transitions.
2. Make it practical to replay stateful layouts and interaction sequences while debugging regressions.
3. Ensure the visual-debug hooks integrate naturally with the redesign proof harness.

Acceptance:
1. Non-trivial redesigned states can be captured and replayed deterministically.
2. The proof harness can consume these hooks when diagnosing regressions.
3. Future maintainers can use the replay path without reverse-engineering the implementation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.829802832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.829802832Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","diagnostics","frankentui","replay","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.829802832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","title":"Expand the TUI proof harness for the redesign: journeys, responsive tiers, themes, mouse/hit tests, and evidence-rich failures","description":"Purpose:\nTurn the TUI redesign into a durable evidence package that can justify parity claims and catch regressions before they drift back into optimistic storytelling.\n\nBackground:\nThe existing proof story was too easy to read as snapshot stability equals product quality. This redesign needs a layered evidence system: lower-level deterministic proof by screen family plus shell-level journey evidence with artifact bundles and replay paths.\n\nRequired scope:\n1. Coordinate lower-level proof for shell/main, board/graph/insights, and history so every rebuilt surface has deterministic coverage.\n2. Coordinate shell-level capture infrastructure and cross-mode operator journeys with structured logging and replay instructions.\n3. Make failures evidence-rich enough that future maintainers can localize regressions without tribal memory.\n\nAcceptance:\n1. The redesign has a complete testing ladder from unit/state through snapshots, keyflows, and shell-level journeys.\n2. Evidence is organized by screen family and by E2E artifact package, not hidden in a single generic test bucket.\n3. Final docs and parity-verdict beads can rely directly on this evidence package.","design":"Proof architecture:\n1. Lower-level proof must be split by screen family so workflow-specific regressions are obvious.\n2. Shell-level proof must separate artifact-harness plumbing from the operator journeys that consume it.\n3. Every layer must emit high-signal diagnostics: mode, width tier, theme, step, expected outcome, and replay/artifact reference.\n4. This epic should make it impossible to claim redesign completion without concrete evidence across all rebuilt modes.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The proof harness explicitly covers unit/state, snapshots, keyflows, journey tests, and shell-level capture artifacts.\n2. Child beads partition the proof burden into coherent, implementable work packages.\n3. Logging and artifact expectations are explicit enough that failures can be debugged offline.\n4. The final parity-verdict work can cite this epic as the canonical redesign evidence package.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:47.154140047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:20:39.919405085Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","journeys","keyflow","snapshots","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:47.154140047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:08.510083902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:06.318022285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:31.545257572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:31.942490137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:06.760347088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:07.193908999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:07.627542976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:08.058443094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.1","title":"Add redesign-focused unit/state, snapshot, and keyflow proof for every rebuilt TUI surface","description":"Purpose:\nCoordinate the lower-level redesign proof family so every rebuilt surface has explicit unit, snapshot, keyflow, and hit-region coverage with diagnostic failures.\n\nBackground:\nBefore this pass, proof planning was broad enough that shell/main, board/graph/insights, and history could have ended up competing for one vague testing bead, and mouse-aware interaction proof could still have slipped through the cracks. This epic prevents that by splitting the lower-level proof burden into concrete screen-family deliverables plus explicit hit-region validation.\n\nRequired scope:\n1. Ensure shell and flagship-main proof is explicit and rich enough to protect the first-run experience.\n2. Ensure board, graph, and insights each receive mode-specific lower-level proof rather than sharing generic snapshot coverage.\n3. Ensure history investigation behaviors get their own stateful proof for file-tree, confidence, and jump continuity.\n4. Ensure mouse and hit-region behavior is explicitly validated for high-value interactive controls.\n5. Standardize failure metadata so lower-level proof always reports mode, width tier, theme, step, and operator contract.\n\nAcceptance:\n1. Lower-level redesign proof is split into concrete screen-family beads rather than one generic bucket.\n2. Every major rebuilt surface is covered by deterministic unit/state, snapshot, keyflow, and where relevant hit-region obligations.\n3. Logging and assertion messages are consistently diagnostic across the proof family.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat this epic as the coordinator for lower-level proof obligations, not as a place to hide testing vagueness.\n2. Keep proof close to the user-visible workflow families: shell/main, board/graph/insights, and history.\n3. Prefer deterministic assertions that explain operator expectations over brittle string-freezing.\n4. Align failure metadata across all child beads so later maintainers can reason about regressions quickly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The lower-level proof family is decomposed into screen-specific and interaction-specific beads with clear ownership.\n2. Each child bead names the workflows, width tiers, interaction semantics, and failure metadata it must cover.\n3. The family as a whole protects every rebuilt TUI surface before shell-level E2E is considered complete.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:26.737327336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:57.205109252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","keyflow","snapshots","testing","tui","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:06.251208796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:26.737327336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -78,7 +78,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","title":"Build adjustable splitter behavior, pane allocation state, and resize resilience with deterministic regression coverage","description":"Purpose:
Add the pane-control mechanics that allow multi-panel screens to behave like tools rather than fixed reports.

Required scope:
1. Support splitter-driven pane resizing where it materially improves workflows.
2. Preserve pane allocation state across navigation and resizing where appropriate.
3. Ensure resizing does not strand controls or produce degenerate panel allocations.
4. Provide deterministic tests for splitter math, pane clamping, and resize transitions.

Acceptance:
1. Multi-panel screens can support deliberate pane allocation rather than only hardcoded ratios.
2. Resize behavior remains coherent across repeated terminal size changes.
3. Proof coverage exists for pane-state math and visible behavior.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.523980602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.523980602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","layout","resize","splitters","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.523980602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","title":"Build semantic visual primitives and a stronger theme/token system for high-density triage screens","description":"Purpose:
Create the reusable visual language that the redesigned screens need: semantic panels, badges, chips, metric strips, separators, and stronger palette discipline.

Why this matters:
The current TUI uses a small custom dark palette with limited semantic range. As a result, most screens are visually flat even when they contain useful information. If the redesign keeps adding new behavior without improving the visual primitives, the result will still feel bare.

Required scope:
- Define neutral surface tokens and accent/focus/status tokens with clear semantic roles.
- Add reusable UI primitives for status chips, priority badges, metric strips, rank badges, command chips, panel headers, and section dividers.
- Support stronger section delineation than generic bordered paragraphs, including rounded or semantically accented panel treatments where appropriate.
- Make sure these primitives work across themes and widths.

Considerations:
- The goal is not random ornament. Every visual primitive should improve scanability or affordance recognition.
- High-chroma accents should be reserved for focus, action, risk, or state, while larger surfaces remain more neutral.
- This bead should establish the vocabulary that every rebuilt mode uses so the TUI feels like one product.

Acceptance:
- Reusable primitives exist for the major recurring information types in `bvr`.
- The new primitives materially improve hierarchy and density without producing visual noise.
- Later mode-specific beads can depend on this bead instead of reinventing styling locally.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Build a shared semantic token system that separates neutral surfaces, focus states, risk states, status states, and action accents.\n2. Prefer reusable chips, badges, metric strips, and panel headers over per-screen ad hoc styling.\n3. Treat theme support as a product feature, not an implementation afterthought.\n4. Design visual primitives so they survive narrow widths and theme changes without losing meaning.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is where the redesign stops looking like more text in more boxes and starts becoming a coherent terminal product.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Shared visual primitives exist for the recurring concepts in  and are used by downstream screen beads.\n2. Theme and palette behavior is explicit enough that later shells and screens do not need to invent one-off colors.\n3. Visual hierarchy is materially stronger than the current bordered-paragraph baseline.\n4. Unit/render tests cover the new primitives, and visual/snapshot proof exists for at least a representative set of tokens and components across size tiers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.194969456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.864722306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","frankentui","theme","tui","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.194969456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:48.245129740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:57.837266604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:58.238914750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.1","title":"Define the semantic theme and token contract for redesigned TUI surfaces and states","description":"Purpose:
Define the shared semantic token vocabulary for surfaces, focus, state, risk, and action so later screens do not invent incompatible styling.

Required scope:
1. Map neutral surfaces, active/focused surfaces, risk states, status states, and action accents to an explicit token contract.
2. Ensure the contract is usable across multiple themes and width tiers.
3. Encode enough semantics that mode rebuilds can style themselves consistently without hardcoded local colors.

Acceptance:
1. The redesign has an explicit semantic token contract rather than a bag of ad hoc colors.
2. Theme behavior is coherent and testable.
3. Later screen beads can depend on this contract rather than redefining it.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.869327876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.869327876Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","frankentui","theme","tokens","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.869327876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","title":"Build reusable visual primitives: chips, badges, metric strips, semantic panels, and command hints with regression proof","description":"Purpose:
Create the shared presentational building blocks that let the redesigned screens stop rendering themselves as giant strings in generic boxes.

Required scope:
1. Add reusable primitives for status chips, priority badges, metric strips, panel headers, command chips, and section separators.
2. Add semantic panel treatments strong enough to support cockpit, analytics, graph, and investigation layouts.
3. Make these components narrow-width-safe and theme-aware.
4. Add render/snapshot coverage so the primitives themselves are stable and understandable.

Acceptance:
1. Rebuilt screens can be composed from shared primitives instead of bespoke text formatting.
2. The visual primitives materially improve hierarchy and scanability.
3. Component-level proof exists for the new primitives.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.209926749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:04:44.361899671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","frankentui","testing","tui","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.209926749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Implemented 9 reusable visual primitives in src/tui.rs with 12 regression tests:

2. priority_badge() — coloured P0-P4 with clamping for out-of-range values
3. type_badge() — single-letter type icon (T/B/E/F/Q/D)
4. metric_strip() — compact label + mini bar + value display
5. blocker_indicator() — shows blocked count or unblocks count with colour coding
6. section_separator() — dim horizontal rule with 120-char max width cap
7. panel_header() — bold title with optional dim subtitle
8. label_chips() — bracketed coloured label tags inline
9. issue_scan_line() — dense single-line issue summary composing all of the above

+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","title":"Build reusable visual primitives: chips, badges, metric strips, semantic panels, and command hints with regression proof","description":"Purpose:
Create the shared presentational building blocks that let the redesigned screens stop rendering themselves as giant strings in generic boxes.

Required scope:
1. Add reusable primitives for status chips, priority badges, metric strips, panel headers, command chips, and section separators.
2. Add semantic panel treatments strong enough to support cockpit, analytics, graph, and investigation layouts.
3. Make these components narrow-width-safe and theme-aware.
4. Add render/snapshot coverage so the primitives themselves are stable and understandable.

Acceptance:
1. Rebuilt screens can be composed from shared primitives instead of bespoke text formatting.
2. The visual primitives materially improve hierarchy and scanability.
3. Component-level proof exists for the new primitives.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.209926749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:31:53.143997314Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:31:53.143467020Z","close_reason":"Wired all visual primitive families into live TUI surfaces in src/tui.rs and added focused runtime regression proof.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","frankentui","testing","tui","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.209926749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Implemented 9 reusable visual primitives in src/tui.rs with 12 regression tests:

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","title":"Build grapheme-safe text, hyperlink, and terminal-native interaction primitives for the redesigned TUI","description":"Purpose:
Add the text-measurement and terminal-native interaction foundations that a flagship TUI needs before we scale up the redesign.

Background:
The current TUI is string-heavy. That makes it vulnerable to Unicode width bugs, truncation mistakes, and inconsistent wrapping as screens become richer. It also leaves terminal-native affordances like OSC-8 hyperlinks underused even though `bvr` has many natural link targets: issue IDs, repos, commits, docs, and external references.

Required scope:
- Adopt grapheme-safe, display-width-aware layout and truncation discipline for screen rendering.
- Add richer wrapping/truncation helpers appropriate for dense panels and narrow layouts.
- Introduce hyperlink handling for issue/detail/history/graph contexts where terminal links materially improve usability.
- Make links keyboard- and mouse-accessible rather than treating them as dead text.

Why it belongs early:
- It is easier to get width correctness and link affordances right before all screens are rebuilt.
- History and graph especially will benefit from terminal-native navigation once they stop being plain reports.

Acceptance:
- The redesign has a clear text-metrics policy suitable for Unicode-safe terminal rendering.
- Link-worthy entities can be rendered as actual terminal links where appropriate.
- Subsequent screen rebuilds can depend on these primitives rather than hacking one-off width logic.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Use display-width- and grapheme-aware text handling for layout-sensitive rendering paths.\n2. Avoid byte-length assumptions for truncation, wrapping, cursor placement, or alignment.\n3. Introduce terminal-native links only where they clearly improve operator flow, and ensure keyboard/mouse focus states are coherent.\n4. Keep the text and link primitives reusable across main, graph, and history surfaces.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead protects the redesign from subtle regressions that are easy to miss in ASCII-heavy terminals until users hit unusual titles, labels, repos, or external refs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The redesign has shared grapheme-safe truncation, wrapping, and alignment helpers suitable for dense panels.\n2. Link-capable surfaces can render real terminal links where appropriate without degrading keyboard-first workflows.\n3. Unit tests cover tricky Unicode and width cases.\n4. Integration or journey coverage proves that link and text affordances behave correctly inside at least one real screen.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.493702553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:38:45.873746453Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T03:38:45.873145876Z","close_reason":"All 4 acceptance criteria met. AC1: 6+ shared grapheme-safe helpers (fit_display, center_display, wrap_command_hints, truncate_display, display_width, command_hint_width). AC2: OSC-8 hyperlinks in Main/Graph/History with keyboard (o/y) and mouse (click/right-click) actions. AC3: 6 Unicode width tests (CJK double-width, emoji ZWJ, grapheme clusters). AC4: 7 integration tests proving link+text affordances in real screens (hyperlink rendering, mouse click/copy, detail scrolling). Multiple agents contributed over 5 days.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","frankentui","links","text","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.493702553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:48.609538600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"BlueRobin","text":"First concrete bd-2bpl.3.6.5 slice landed in src/tui.rs: added reusable CommandHint plus width-aware wrap_command_hints()/command_hint_line() helpers, and switched main/history footer hint generation onto styled RichText command hints instead of one raw ASCII sentence. The wrapping logic now packs whole hint groups by display width so narrow terminals get clean multi-line hint clusters. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib wrap_command_hints_preserves_groups_and_styles ; cargo test --lib main_footer_command_hints_wrap_across_multiple_lines ; cargo check --all-targets ; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:04:54Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"BlueRobin","text":"Extended the active bd-2bpl.3.6.5 slice in src/tui.rs by moving terminal-native links onto a real operator surface. Implementation: main_detail_scroll_offset field, scroll_main_detail() method, Paragraph.scroll() rendering, key handlers, selection-change reset. Added 5 regression tests. Updated 4 snapshots (help overlay, history git mode, 2 journey tests).","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:39:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","title":"Rebuild the flagship main triage surface as a cockpit-grade operator view","description":"Purpose:
Turn the default screen into the flagship experience that proves the redesign is real. This is the screen that must immediately feel like a serious triage console rather than a formatted report.

Background:
The audit found that the main screen contains useful data, but its scan line is too thin, its detail pane is too report-like, and its discoverability is overly dependent on footer prose. Because this is the entry screen, that weakness shapes the entire first impression of `bvr`.

Required scope:
- Redesign the list rows to surface far more decision-critical information at a glance.
- Recompose the detail pane into strong modules with explicit visual hierarchy and action orientation.
- Upgrade main-screen search, filter, focus, and discovery affordances so the screen is fast in the first 3 seconds, not only after a user learns the keymap.

Design intent:
- This screen should establish the visual and interaction patterns that secondary modes then inherit or specialize.
- Better-than-legacy improvements are allowed, but only if the legacy decision loop becomes faster and clearer, not more ornamental.

Acceptance:
- A user can decide what to inspect or act on with far less scanning effort than in the current build.
- The screen reads as a cockpit with modules, not a single large report.
- Search/filter/focus behavior is visibly integrated into the product rather than hidden in text hints.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. This is the flagship bead. If tradeoffs are required, prioritize the first-run triage experience over secondary mode polish.\n2. The rebuilt main view must use the shell, layout, and visual primitives from earlier beads rather than bypassing them.\n3. Every major interaction improvement should be encoded into deterministic tests so the flagship screen does not regress later.\n\nFuture-self note: If this bead lands and still feels like a report viewer, the redesign is not actually working. This is the screen that must prove the new architecture pays off.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The rebuilt main screen is materially denser, clearer, and faster to scan than the current implementation.\n2. The list and detail panes both use the redesigned visual/system primitives instead of falling back to large text dumps.\n3. Search, filter, focus, and discoverability improvements are integrated into the workflow, not only documented.\n4. Unit/state tests, keyflow coverage, snapshots for width tiers, and at least one multi-step journey test all encode the new flagship behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.790319632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.877464338Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","main-view","redesign","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:27.430340706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.790319632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:48.973512105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:00.254493923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:49.382571631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:01.091617834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:49.737567146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:01.507934232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.095812428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","title":"Redesign main-screen rows with dense scan-line chips, rank signals, blocker context, and stronger information scent","description":"Purpose:
Make the main list itself do real triage work before the operator ever opens the detail pane.

Background:
Today the main rows mostly expose ID, status, priority, and title. That leaves the operator blind to many of the cues that actually drive prioritization: blocker load, unblocks count, age/staleness, assignee, repo, urgency, and rank/score context.

Required scope:
- Replace the current low-information row format with a compact but legible scan line built from semantic chips/badges and aligned signals.
- Surface enough context that a user can compare items without repeatedly opening the detail pane.
- Preserve readability at narrow widths by using responsive row variants rather than just truncating everything.

Acceptance:
- Main-list rows communicate materially more useful triage information than the current implementation.
- Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes.
- The result improves scanability without becoming visually noisy.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat each row as a triage decision surface, not a decorative summary line.\n2. Encode rank, blocker load, ownership, freshness, and scope cues so users can compare items without opening the detail pane repeatedly.\n3. Define distinct row behavior for crowded, filtered, and no-hit contexts so the list still teaches the operator what happened.\n4. Keep the row system stable under long titles, long labels, Unicode, and dense datasets by relying on the shared text-width and visual-primitives work.\n\nFuture-self note: If the rows only look denser in screenshots but still force constant detail-pane opening, this bead missed the point.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Main-list rows communicate materially more useful triage information than the current implementation.\n2. Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes, dense datasets, and long/Unicode content.\n3. Filtered, empty, and no-hit list states remain legible and recovery-oriented rather than collapsing into dead space.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and keyflow coverage prove row behavior for dense datasets, filtered states, and degraded-width variants.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:24:38.443227999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["density","frankentui","main-view","triage","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.454890035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.812307267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
